scores.note: candidate_floor not yet measured — apply candidate.c to src/display.c then run sandbox to record. Expected to be > 0 (the score-0 form was the FAIL form with sp[4] coercion). The legitimate base may score similar to HEAD's 24 or lower; record the measurement when known.

sessions.lever: inverted-null-check (`if (arg0 != NULL) { body; return X; } return 0;`) + m2c-shape body + r_e2 named intermediate + temp_v0 var-reuse — derived to replace the forbidden goto-end-prologue-delay-slot accumulator from a prior session
sessions.note: Score-0 measurement on 2026-06-01 was on the FAIL form (with sp[4] coercion). The legitimate base — same form WITHOUT sp[4] — was not measured before the revert. Next session: apply candidate.c, measure, record.

sessions.lever: workflow round 1: structural re-analysis of target asm; considered and rejected read+write array and struct-member variants
sessions.note: Target genuinely emits 4 sw stores plus a 0x10 frame allocation; stores are dead. Considered an array variant referenced via reads in return and struct-member variant. Both fail human-programmer test and naming-intent test. Per default-to-NO_PROGRESS rule, rejected. Could not measure candidate in isolated worktree. Worker suggested escalating for canonical-asm authorization analogous to gte-wrapper-misroute-park (the 4 dead frame writes are not pure-C-reproducible without a coercion construct).

reviewer.evidence: Independent cheat-reviewer agent FAILED commit 0e845d2 (the sp[4] write-only frame coercion) on Tests 1 (no semantic purpose), 2 (human-programmer test), and 6 (naming announces intent). The candidate.c form (this WIP) REMOVES the sp[4] writes — the inverted-null-check + m2c body + r_e2 + var-reuse parts are SOTN-allowed and would not draw the same FAIL. Verdict needs re-running on the candidate as a fresh review before any Match: commit.

remaining_gap.summary: Unknown — needs first-pass measurement on the legitimate-base candidate. The prior FAIL form happened to reach SHA1 == oracle via the sp[4] coercion controlling the frame size; the legitimate base will likely diverge on frame layout (since sp[4] was reserving frame bytes). The next session's task is to determine what gap remains and find a SOTN-clean lever that closes it without re-introducing forbidden frame-coercion constructs.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: Same 9 regfix substs as HEAD (regfix.txt — they were dropped by retire in 0e845d2, but restored by the revert). New shape may need different rules; un-knowable without measurement.

next_hypotheses: Escalate for canonical-asm authorization analogous to gte-wrapper-misroute-park. The 4 dead frame writes are not pure-C-reproducible without a coercion construct. NOTE: per .claude/rules/hand-coded-asm-recognition.md strict gate, the canonical-asm option needs explicit signal-evidence (S1/S2/S6/S8) — the worker did NOT confirm hand-coded signals; this is a request for user adjudication, not an auto-authorize category. Compare against the B844 standard (B844's worker raised the same canonical-asm option in session 3 and the WIP user_policy_note documented why it failed the evidence threshold).
Apply candidate.c to src/display.c, measure sandbox --disable all, record candidate_floor in meta.json.

If candidate_floor is below HEAD's 24, identify what's still gapping. Common levers: frame-layout-natural (without coercion), narrow integer types (s16/u16/u8 typing of temp_v* might shift codegen), declaration-order reshuffling, m2c-style sub-expressions extracted into named intermediates.
ALLOCDBG-instrumented dump on the candidate to identify if a register-allocation tiebreaker is what's blocking.
Directed permuter from the candidate base with cheat-reviewer gating on output. The score-0 sp[4] form was a cheat-disguised-as-match; permuter output MUST be vetted before any commit.
Write-only-array detector (engine/volatile_cheats.py) is a separate work item filed in the revert commit message — closing that gap mechanically prevents future re-derivation of the rejected form.

rejected_forms.form: s32 sp[4]; sp[0..3] = temp_*; (write-only frame coercion)
rejected_forms.reason: FORBIDDEN: write-only-array variant of the dead-vars-local-array family. Detected by independent cheat-reviewer on Tests 1, 2, 6 — zero semantic purpose, no human programmer would write this, 'sp' announces coercion intent. Same family as `s32 buf[N];` (caught by find_unused_local_arrays) but with stores to suppress 'unused-variable' diagnostics. The mechanical detector missed it because 'sp' IS referenced (just being written). Form preserved at rejected/sp4_frame_coercion.c with the reviewer's evidence inline.

rejected_forms.form: Array variant referenced via reads in return (read+write array — alternative shape to the rejected sp[4] write-only)
rejected_forms.reason: Considered as a structural alternative to the previously-rejected sp[4] write-only coercion. Fails human-programmer test and naming-intent test — no pure-C author would emit 4 dead frame stores through an array that's read-once for return value just to bend the prologue. Same family as dead-vars-local-array, just spelled with a read-tap. NOT MEASURED — rejected on review-discipline-before-commit / default-to-NO_PROGRESS grounds. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: Struct-member variant (4 dead stores via local struct members)
rejected_forms.reason: Considered as an alternative shape. Same default-to-NO_PROGRESS rejection: fails human-programmer test and naming-intent test. Local struct declared, members written, read once for return — same coercion-spelled-differently family as the rejected sp[4]. NOT MEASURED. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)
